message:
test(core): add graph IR operations unit tests (bd-1c5.6)

Add 18 unit tests for core graph IR operations:
- Node add/query with shapes and labels
- Edge creation with endpoint types (Node, Port, Unresolved)
- Endpoint resolution through ports
- Label interning and lookup
- Cluster membership tracking
- Subgraph parent-child hierarchy (root/leaf queries)
- Graph IR node/edge/cluster/subgraph queries
- Subgraph key lookup (single and duplicate keys)
- Node-to-cluster and node-to-subgraph associations
- Full IR serde JSON roundtrip preserving all fields
- Diagnostic add/filter/has_errors
- Implicit node flag
- Edge kind and node kind variant distinctness

95 total fm-core tests passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1fef","title":"EPIC: Constraint Programming for Layout (§9.7)","description":"## Constraint Programming for Layout (§9.7)

Formulate diagram layout as a constraint satisfaction/optimization problem and solve with a CP solver, enabling layouts that respect complex user-specified constraints (alignment, spacing, grouping, ordering) that are difficult or impossible to express in traditional Sugiyama/force-directed algorithms.

## Motivation
Traditional layout algorithms optimize a single objective (crossing minimization, edge length) with limited constraint support. Real diagrams have rich constraints:
- "These 3 nodes must be horizontally aligned"
- "This subgraph must be contained in a box with 20px padding"
- "These edges must not overlap this label"
- "Minimum 40px vertical spacing between ranks"
- "This node must be to the left of that node"

Constraint programming encodes ALL of these as first-class constraints and finds a feasible (or optimal) assignment of coordinates. This is the natural formulation for layout-with-constraints.

## Mathematical Foundation
**CP formulation for 2D graph layout:**
- Variables: x_i, y_i in R for each node i (continuous domain)
- Hard constraints: non-overlap (disjunctive), rank ordering for DAG edges, alignment, containment, min spacing
- Soft constraints / objective: minimize total edge length, minimize crossings, minimize bounding box area

**Solver approach:** Mixed Integer Programming (MIP) for the disjunctive constraints (non-overlap requires binary indicator variables), or Satisfiability Modulo Theories (SMT) with linear real arithmetic (LRA) for the continuous constraints.

## Key Papers
- Dwyer, Koren & Marriott, "IPSep-CoLa" (IEEE InfoVis 2006)
- Gansner, Koren & North, "Graph Drawing by Stress Majorization" (GD 2004)
- Chimani et al., "Exact Crossing Minimization via ILP" (Math Programming 2012)
- Schulze & von Hanxleden, "Drawing Layered Graphs with Port Constraints" (JVLC 2014)

## Graveyard Reference: §9.7 -- Constraint Programming for Layout
Score: 3.5 (Alien uplift). Risk: solver performance on large graphs, complexity of constraint modeling.

## Success Metrics
- User-specified alignment, grouping, and ordering constraints respected in layout output
- Layout quality (edge length, crossings, compactness) >= Sugiyama baseline
- Solve time < 1 second for graphs up to 200 nodes with 10 constraints
- Solve time < 5 seconds for graphs up to 500 nodes with 5 constraints
- Graceful timeout: if solver exceeds budget, return Sugiyama fallback with constraint violations annotated

## Risk Mitigation
- MIP solvers can have unpredictable runtime. Use time limit + fallback.
- Consider lightweight constraint propagation (arc consistency) as a pre-filter before full MIP.
- Start with a simple constraint set (alignment + ordering) before tackling non-overlap (which requires binary variables).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:39:15.117285541Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:29:55.413452073Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-cs","constraint-programming","layout","optimization"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1fef","depends_on_id":"bd-17e4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:29:55.413396428Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1fef","depends_on_id":"bd-3bc.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:23:16.687469437Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
